text: Artificial intelligence (AI) research has long been fueled by advances in neuroscience. Now, scientists argue that the field needs more “basic science” on neuro-computing, or NeuroAI research. They say that for real AI advancements to occur, embodied Turing test “animal-like” robots that can navigate and interact with the environment at the same level of proficiency as real animals need to be created. The embodied Turing test moves away from focusing on skills to which humans and computers are naturally predisposed such as playing games or speaking human language and instead towards other abilities that are evolutionary shared among all types of animals for over half a billion years. Passing the test would give AI researchers an idea of the characteristics of future AI systems.
headline: Fully Human Written
